title: Hypertrophic scar review
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports distinguishing mature scars, hypertrophic scars, keloids, and mimics; documenting scar growth within or beyond the original wound, shape, elevation, redness, hardness, progression, risk context, visual inspection, palpation, and diagnostic uncertainty.

source.title: Diagnosis and Treatment of Keloids and Hypertrophic Scars—Japan Scar Workshop Consensus Document 2018
source.jurisdiction: Japan; international scar-consensus evidence requiring UAE adaptation
source.population: People with a possible or established keloid or hypertrophic scar; the document highlights greater susceptibility in Asian and African populations.
source.setting: Primary care, dermatology, plastic-surgery, and specialist scar assessment.
source.exclusions: Exact for documenting scar origin and course, location, symptoms, clinical appearance, growth relative to the original wound, and clinician-assessed scar severity. It does not authorise automatic diagnosis, scoring, biopsy, treatment, procedure, referral, or follow-up.

exact_location.locator: publisher full text, Main context, lines 132–186

assessment.text: Supports recording a clinician-established scar type, subjective symptoms, lesion location, severity context, prior response, movement or tension context, and completed specialist assessment without mapping any treatment instruction.
assessment.rationale: The exact assessment points support recording clinician-assessed severity, subjective symptoms, functional effect, lesion location and prior response; no score, diagnosis, treatment, or referral is generated.
